behaviors.description: The clause extractor identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on semantic extraction quality from a real LLM/model step; mocked outputs can hide missed or misclassified terms, especially in varied legal phrasing.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause-extractor model on a curated set of contracts with human-labeled key-term ground truth (including paraphrased obligations and implied penalties) and assert recall/precision thresholds by term type.

behaviors.description: The risk assessor flags language that would be problematic under the configured risk policy, even when the problematic phrasing is indirect or euphemistic.
behaviors.justification: This is model-judgment quality (policy interpretation over indirect wording). In-process mocks would likely pass while real model behavior can miss euphemisms or over/under-flag.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real risk-assessor model against a labeled adversarial corpus containing direct and euphemistic risky clauses under the configured policy, and verify detection quality metrics and error bounds.

behaviors.description: The compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all applicable regulatory requirements for the configured jurisdiction.
behaviors.justification: Determining compliance from contract text is a semantic legal-classification task where correctness depends on model output quality, not just wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real compliance-checker model on jurisdiction-specific contracts with expert-labeled compliance outcomes (including edge cases and partial compliance) and assert target accuracy/F1 per requirement category.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments, the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Real failure risk is in module/interface wiring and schema reconciliation between reviewer outputs. A unit test of merge logic alone may miss serialization/normalization mismatches across components.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real reviewer-output adapters (or recorded real output payloads) into the merge module and verify that conflicting clause entries are both retained with correct reviewer attribution and no overwrite.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline dispatches to all three reviewers in parallel, collects results, and merges into a unified report without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This depends on multi-step agentic sequencing (parallel AI reviewer calls + collection + merge). Failures can emerge only across the full run (timing, fan-in, partial drops), which mocks or single-step tests can hide.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end pipeline session on real contracts with all three real reviewer calls enabled in parallel; assert completion, presence of each reviewer’s findings in final report, and no dropped/conflicted entries under repeated runs.
